clusters.title: Social Media Ban For Under-16s
clusters.neutral_headline: UK announces social media ban for under-16s
bullet_summary: Children under 16 will be banned from using social media in the UK, Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced on Monday, saying such platforms were making youngsters "unhappy"
The ban will "include platforms like Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook and X" but not messaging services such as WhatsApp, the government said
The prime minister announced that under-16s will be banned from social media by spring 2027
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced a sweeping ban on social media use for those under 16, joining other countries around the world seeking to protect children online

items.title: Social Media Ban For Under-16s
items.standfirst: Under-16s to be banned from social media Keir Starmer announces.
items.content: Under-16s to be banned from social media Keir Starmer announces.

items.title: U.K. announces plan to ban social media for children under 16
items.standfirst: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer hopes to have a ban on kids using social media that is "designed to be addictive" enacted by early next year.
items.content: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer hopes to have a ban on kids using social media that is "designed to be addictive" enacted by early next year.

items.title: UK to ban social media for under-16s
items.standfirst: Children under 16 will be banned from using social media in the UK, Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced on Monday, saying such platforms were making youngsters "unhappy". The ban will "include platforms like Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook and X" but not messaging services such as WhatsApp, the government said.
items.content: Children under 16 will be banned from using social media in the UK, Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced on Monday, saying such platforms were making youngsters "unhappy". The ban will "include platforms like Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook and X" but not messaging services such as WhatsApp, the government said.

items.title: 'Positive' or 'unnecessary'? UK teens on social media ban
items.standfirst: The prime minister announced that under-16s will be banned from social media by spring 2027.
items.content: The prime minister announced that under-16s will be banned from social media by spring 2027.

items.title: How the UK Plans to Keep Children Off Social Media
items.standfirst: The UK has become the latest country to announce a ban on under-16s using social media, following a landmark law in Australia that underscored the growing pressure on governments to better protect children from online harms.
items.content: The UK has become the latest country to announce a ban on under-16s using social media, following a landmark law in Australia that underscored the growing pressure on governments to better protect children from online harms.

items.title: UK announces U16 social media ban
items.standfirst: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer announces a ban for under-16s from major social media platforms
items.content: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer announces a ban for under-16s from major social media platforms

items.title: How will social media ban work and when does it start?
items.standfirst: The measures will see apps including TikTok and Snapchat banned for UK teens early in 2027.
items.content: The measures will see apps including TikTok and Snapchat banned for UK teens early in 2027.

items.title: UK announces social media ban for under-16s

items.content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer says the UK will ban social media for teens under the age of 16.

items.title: Britain will ban under-16s from social media apps, including TikTok and YouTube
items.standfirst: The ban will apply to platforms including Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook and X. The move makes the U.K. part of a growing global movement to tighten online safety for children.
items.content: The ban will apply to platforms including Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook and X. The move makes the U.K. part of a growing global movement to tighten online safety for children.(Image credit: Carlos Jasso)

items.title: Britain announces sweeping social media ban for under-16s
items.standfirst: PM warns social media platforms are exposing children to content that is 'dangerous' and 'designed to be addictive'.
items.content: PM warns social media platforms are exposing children to content that is 'dangerous' and 'designed to be addictive'.

items.title: The UK Places a Sweeping Ban on Social Media for Kids Under 16
items.standfirst: The UK government is introducing a ban on social media for children and a minimum age for some chatbots in an attempt to shield young people from dangerous corners of the web.
items.content: The UK government is introducing a ban on social media for children and a minimum age for some chatbots in an attempt to shield young people from dangerous corners of the web.

items.title: Britain unveils sweeping ban on social media for under-16s
items.standfirst: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced a sweeping ban on social media use for those under 16, joining other countries around the world seeking to protect children online.
items.content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced a sweeping ban on social media use for those under 16, joining other countries around the world seeking to protect children online.

items.title: UK Seeks One of World’s Strictest Social Media Bans for Kids
items.standfirst: Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced what he described as a “full ban” on children using social media, going beyond limits imposed by Australia last year to create one of the strictest online crackdowns in the democratic world.
items.content: Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced what he described as a “full ban” on children using social media, going beyond limits imposed by Australia last year to create one of the strictest online crackdowns in the democratic world.

items.title: Trump lands in Switzerland for G7 Summit as world awaits details on Iran deal
items.standfirst: President Trump landed in Geneva, Switzerland, on Monday for the G7 Summit. CBS News' Nancy Cordes has the latest as more questions loom about the U.S.-Iran deal.
items.content: President Trump landed in Geneva, Switzerland, on Monday for the G7 Summit. CBS News' Nancy Cordes has the latest as more questions loom about the U.S.-Iran deal.

items.title: Hegseth on U.S.-Iran deal: "Military might will stay as long as necessary"
items.standfirst: President Trump said the Strait of Hormuz will reopen on Friday after a U.S.-Iran deal is signed, adding the U.S. is ending its naval blockade. Mr. Trump is attending the G7 Summit ahead of the planned deal signing ceremony. CBS News' Nancy Cordes and Courtney Kealy have the latest details of the deal.

items.title: Trump says U.S.-Iran deal ‘complete’ after months of negotiations
items.standfirst: A deal between the United States and Iran has been reached, President Donald Trump announced Sunday. The President added that the U.S. naval blockade would be removed from the Strait of Hormuz. NBC News’ Keir Simmons has the latest.
items.content: A deal between the United States and Iran has been reached, President Donald Trump announced Sunday. The President added that the U.S. naval blockade would be removed from the Strait of Hormuz. NBC News’ Keir Simmons has the latest.

items.title: Deal between U.S., Iran has been reached says Pakistan's prime minister
items.standfirst: Prime Minister of Pakistan Shehbaz Sharif announced that a deal between the U.S. and Iran has been reached with a signing expected on Friday in Switzerland. NBC News' Keir Simmons, Gabe Gutierrez and Matt Bradley report.
items.content: Prime Minister of Pakistan Shehbaz Sharif announced that a deal between the U.S. and Iran has been reached with a signing expected on Friday in Switzerland. NBC News' Keir Simmons, Gabe Gutierrez and Matt Bradley report.

bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Guardian World, France24

items.title: Norwegian crown princess's son found guilty of two counts of rape
items.standfirst: Marius Borg Høiby, the 29-year-old son of Norway's Crown Princess Mette Marit, has been sentenced to four years in jail.
items.content: Marius Borg Høiby, the 29-year-old son of Norway's Crown Princess Mette Marit, has been sentenced to four years in jail.

items.title: Son of Norway’s crown princess convicted of rape and sentenced to four years in prison
items.standfirst: Marius Borg Høiby found guilty of ⁠two ​counts of rape, one count of domestic violence and other ⁠crimesMarius Borg Høiby, the son of Norway’s crown princess, has been sentenced to four years in prison after being found guilty of several offences including two counts of rape.The verdict was handed down by the Oslo district court on Monday morning in a courthouse packed with journalists, nearly three months after Høiby’s closely watched six-week trial. Continue reading...
items.content: Marius Borg Høiby found guilty of ⁠two ​counts of rape, one count of domestic violence and other ⁠crimesMarius Borg Høiby, the son of Norway’s crown princess, has been sentenced to four years in prison after being found guilty of several offences including two counts of rape.The verdict was handed down by the Oslo district court on Monday morning in a courthouse packed with journalists, nearly three months after Høiby’s closely watched six-week trial. Continue reading...

items.title: Son of Norway's crown princess sentenced to four years in prison for rape
items.standfirst: Marius Borg Høiby, the son of Norway’s crown princess, was sentenced to four years in prison on Monday after an Oslo court convicted him of two counts of rape and 32 other offences, including violence against a former partner, threats and drug-related crimes.
items.content: Marius Borg Høiby, the son of Norway’s crown princess, was sentenced to four years in prison on Monday after an Oslo court convicted him of two counts of rape and 32 other offences, including violence against a former partner, threats and drug-related crimes.

items.title: Norway braces for verdict in rape trial of crown princess's son Høiby
items.standfirst: Marius Borg Høiby will appear via video link, almost three months after his trial came to an end on 40 charges, including four counts of rape.
items.content: Marius Borg Høiby will appear via video link, almost three months after his trial came to an end on 40 charges, including four counts of rape.

clusters.neutral_headline: Kyiv monastery set on fire in night of Russian attacks across Ukraine
bullet_summary: ”In a statement, the body said:“The strike reportedly caused significant damage to the exterior and interior of the Dormition Cathedral
Adjacent historic structures, including elements of the Lavra’s fortification complex and Ivan Kushnik Tower, were also reportedly impacted
Fire engulfed the Kyiv Perchersk Lavra monastery, a UNESCO World Heritage site founded in 1051, after a massive Russian missile and drone attack on Ukraine’s capital

items.title: Ukraine: Kyiv's historic cathedral set ablaze in Russian attack
items.standfirst: One of #Ukraine's most important religious landmarks was hit in an overnight Russian attack on June 15. Ukrainian President Volodymyr #Zelensky visited the cathedral and called for more pressure on Russian President Vladimir #Putin ahead of his attendance at the #G7 summit in #Paris.
items.content: One of #Ukraine's most important religious landmarks was hit in an overnight Russian attack on June 15. Ukrainian President Volodymyr #Zelensky visited the cathedral and called for more pressure on Russian President Vladimir #Putin ahead of his attendance at the #G7 summit in #Paris.

items.title: Zelenskyy says Russia ‘deliberately’ targeted Unesco site, as 11 people confirmed dead – as it happened
items.standfirst: Ukraine’s president dismisses Moscow’s claim that it did not target Kyiv’s Pechersk Lavra monastery, ahead of meeting of G7 leaders in FranceKyiv monastery set on fire in night of Russian attacks across UkraineMacron frames Évian G7 agenda in hope Trump will stay for whole summitMeanwhile, Unesco has formally condemned the Russian strikes on Ukraine that hit the Kyiv-Pechersk Lavra, “one of Ukraine’s most significant spiritual and cultural landmarks.”In a statement, the body said:“The strike reportedly caused significant damage to the exterior and interior of the Dormition Cathedral. Adjacent historic structures, including elements of the Lavra’s fortification complex and Ivan Kushnik Tower, were also reportedly impacted.Unesco condemns attacks against cultural property, educational institutions, students, education personnel and media professionals protected under international law. Damage to such institutions deprives communities of access to culture, education, and shared spaces that are essential for recovery and social cohesion. Continue reading...
items.content: Ukraine’s president dismisses Moscow’s claim that it did not target Kyiv’s Pechersk Lavra monastery, ahead of meeting of G7 leaders in FranceKyiv monastery set on fire in night of Russian attacks across UkraineMacron frames Évian G7 agenda in hope Trump will stay for whole summitMeanwhile, Unesco has formally condemned the Russian strikes on Ukraine that hit the Kyiv-Pechersk Lavra, “one of Ukraine’s most significant spiritual and cultural landmarks.”In a statement, the body said:“The strike reportedly caused significant damage to the exterior and interior of the Dormition Cathedral. Adjacent historic structures, including elements of the Lavra’s fortification complex and Ivan Kushnik Tower, were also reportedly impacted.Unesco condemns attacks against cultural property, educational institutions, students, education personnel and media professionals protected under international law. Damage to such institutions deprives communities of access to culture, education, and shared spaces that are essential for recovery and social cohesion. Continue reading...

items.title: G7 summit: 'Pressure will be on leaders to show support for Ukraine'
items.standfirst: As G7 leaders meet in France this week, Iran and the peace deal signed with the US is likely to be in the spotlight. But with Volodymyr Zelensky in attendance, Ukraine is also set to be a major point of discussion, with leaders expected to show their support for the country in the wake of recent Russian attacks. France 24's Philip Turle has the latest.
items.content: As G7 leaders meet in France this week, Iran and the peace deal signed with the US is likely to be in the spotlight. But with Volodymyr Zelensky in attendance, Ukraine is also set to be a major point of discussion, with leaders expected to show their support for the country in the wake of recent Russian attacks. France 24's Philip Turle has the latest.

items.title: Deadly Russian attacks fuel blaze at centuries-old monastery in Kyiv
items.standfirst: Deadly Russian strikes across Ukraine overnight sparked an intense blaze at a 1,000-year-old monastery in Ukraine’s capital Kyiv, one of the country’s most important religious and cultural landmarks
items.content: Deadly Russian strikes across Ukraine overnight sparked an intense blaze at a 1,000-year-old monastery in Ukraine’s capital Kyiv, one of the country’s most important religious and cultural landmarks

items.title: Kyiv monastery set on fire in night of Russian attacks across Ukraine
items.standfirst: Dormition Cathedral at Unesco world heritage site struck along with residential buildings across capitalEurope live – latest updatesA massive Russian missile and drone attack on Kyiv has badly damaged the Dormition Cathedral in the Pechersk Lavra monastery complex, a Unesco world heritage site and one of Ukraine’s most significant religious and cultural sites.Five people were killed in Kyiv, where waves of drones and missiles drove residents to underground shelters and heavy explosions echoed throughout the capital. Kyiv’s Oleksandr Dovzhenko national film studio, which houses Ukraine’s largest and oldest costume collection, was also hit. Continue reading...
items.content: Dormition Cathedral at Unesco world heritage site struck along with residential buildings across capitalEurope live – latest updatesA massive Russian missile and drone attack on Kyiv has badly damaged the Dormition Cathedral in the Pechersk Lavra monastery complex, a Unesco world heritage site and one of Ukraine’s most significant religious and cultural sites.Five people were killed in Kyiv, where waves of drones and missiles drove residents to underground shelters and heavy explosions echoed throughout the capital. Kyiv’s Oleksandr Dovzhenko national film studio, which houses Ukraine’s largest and oldest costume collection, was also hit. Continue reading...

items.title: Fire engulfs centuries-old Ukrainian monastery after deadly strikes
items.standfirst: Fire engulfed the Kyiv Perchersk Lavra monastery, a UNESCO World Heritage site founded in 1051, after a massive Russian missile and drone attack on Ukraine’s capital. Moscow denied responsibility for the attack but President Zelenskyy described it as “one of Russia’s most serious crimes against Christian culture.”
items.content: Fire engulfed the Kyiv Perchersk Lavra monastery, a UNESCO World Heritage site founded in 1051, after a massive Russian missile and drone attack on Ukraine’s capital. Moscow denied responsibility for the attack but President Zelenskyy described it as “one of Russia’s most serious crimes against Christian culture.”

clusters.title: Arrested protesters devastated after appeal court rules ban on Palestine Action is lawful
clusters.neutral_headline: Ban on Palestine Action was lawful, court of appeal rules
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Al Jazeera, BBC Top Stories

items.title: Arrested protesters devastated after appeal court rules ban on Palestine Action is lawful
items.standfirst: Proscription of direct action group has led to more than 700 people being charged under Terrorism ActProtesters arrested for allegedly supporting Palestine Action have expressed anger at the court of appeal’s decision that the ban on the direct action group was lawful.On Monday, five judges overturned the high court’s February ruling that proscription was unlawful, meaning that more than 3,000 people who have been arrested under the Terrorism Act since proscription, more than 700 of whom have been charged, could now face prosecution. Continue reading...
items.content: Proscription of direct action group has led to more than 700 people being charged under Terrorism ActProtesters arrested for allegedly supporting Palestine Action have expressed anger at the court of appeal’s decision that the ban on the direct action group was lawful.On Monday, five judges overturned the high court’s February ruling that proscription was unlawful, meaning that more than 3,000 people who have been arrested under the Terrorism Act since proscription, more than 700 of whom have been charged, could now face prosecution. Continue reading...

items.title: Ban on Palestine Action was lawful, court of appeal rules
items.standfirst: Judges overturn decision of high court that government proscription of group under Terrorism Act was wrongThe home secretary’s decision to ban Palestine Action was lawful, the court of appeal has ruled.A five-strong panel, including the two most senior judges in England and Wales, overturned February’s decision of the high court that the proscription of the direct action group, the first to be banned under the Terrorism Act, was wrong. Continue reading...
items.content: Judges overturn decision of high court that government proscription of group under Terrorism Act was wrongThe home secretary’s decision to ban Palestine Action was lawful, the court of appeal has ruled.A five-strong panel, including the two most senior judges in England and Wales, overturned February’s decision of the high court that the proscription of the direct action group, the first to be banned under the Terrorism Act, was wrong. Continue reading...

items.content: Britain's Court of Appeal ruled that the government was right to proscribe Palestine Action - what happens next?

clusters.title: Brazil : Oliver Tree, “Missed You” singer, killed in helicopter crash
clusters.neutral_headline: US musician Oliver Tree dies in helicopter collision in Brazil
bullet_summary: Two helicopters collided over Rio de Janeiro on Sunday morning and crashed in the city’s western zone, killing all six people onboard, including Tree, several Brazilian media outlets reported
Reported by France24, BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Brazil : Oliver Tree, “Missed You” singer, killed in helicopter crash
items.standfirst: American alt-pop singer and internet personality Oliver Tree was aboard one of the two helicopters that collided mid-air in Brazil, leaving six dead. The musician was known for hits such as "Miss You" and "Life Goes On." He had over 11 million monthly listeners on Spotify, with his top songs listened to over 700 million times.
items.content: American alt-pop singer and internet personality Oliver Tree was aboard one of the two helicopters that collided mid-air in Brazil, leaving six dead. The musician was known for hits such as "Miss You" and "Life Goes On." He had over 11 million monthly listeners on Spotify, with his top songs listened to over 700 million times.

clusters.title: Swiss reject proposal to cap population at 10 million
clusters.neutral_headline: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Reuters Top News

items.title: Swiss reject proposal to cap population at 10 million
items.standfirst: Voters in Switzerland on Sunday narrowly rejected a divisive anti-immigration proposal to cap the country's population, which had sparked warnings of "chaos". Nearly 55 percent of Swiss voters rejected the initiative put forward by the hard-right Swiss People's Party (SVP), which sought to stop the Alpine nation's population -- currently 9.1 million -- going above 10 million before 2050.
items.content: Voters in Switzerland on Sunday narrowly rejected a divisive anti-immigration proposal to cap the country's population, which had sparked warnings of "chaos". Nearly 55 percent of Swiss voters rejected the initiative put forward by the hard-right Swiss People's Party (SVP), which sought to stop the Alpine nation's population -- currently 9.1 million -- going above 10 million before 2050.

items.title: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap
items.standfirst: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.
items.content: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.

items.title: Swiss voters reject proposal to cap population at ten million
items.standfirst: Swiss voters have rejected a right-wing initiative that sought to limit the population to 10 million, with final results showing a 55% majority against it. The vote also came alongside approval of a civilian service reform, while debate over immigration and Switzerland’s ties with the EU remained central to the campaign. [swissinfo.ch]
items.content: Swiss voters have rejected a right-wing initiative that sought to limit the population to 10 million, with final results showing a 55% majority against it. The vote also came alongside approval of a civilian service reform, while debate over immigration and Switzerland’s ties with the EU remained central to the campaign. [swissinfo.ch]

items.standfirst: Eleven skydivers and a pilot were killed in Sunday crash as aircraft for Skydive Kansas City was taking off in ButlerThe Missouri skydiving community is mourning the loss of several of its members after a plane crash south of Kansas City killed 11 skydivers and a pilot.The crash occurred around noon on Sunday in Butler, Missouri, as an aircraft supporting operations for Skydive Kansas City was taking off, the company said in a statement on Monday, as reported by the local news outlet KCTV. Reported by CBS News, France24

items.title: Trump hosts "surreal" UFC fights at White House and makes history
items.standfirst: President Trump hosted a series of "UFC Freedom 250" mixed martial arts fights at the White House to celebrate America's 250th anniversary on his 80th birthday. CBS News' Weijia Jiang reports on the event and turnout.
items.content: President Trump hosted a series of "UFC Freedom 250" mixed martial arts fights at the White House to celebrate America's 250th anniversary on his 80th birthday. CBS News' Weijia Jiang reports on the event and turnout.

items.title: #Trump celebrates 80th birthday with #UFC fight at #WhiteHouse
items.standfirst: To kick-off celebrations for the United States' 250th anniversary, the #Trump administration hosted a #UFC fight in the White House garden. The $60 million event, which coincided with the US president's 80th birthday, came a few hours after Trump and Iranian officials announced they had reached a preliminary agreement to end the #war.
items.content: To kick-off celebrations for the United States' 250th anniversary, the #Trump administration hosted a #UFC fight in the White House garden. The $60 million event, which coincided with the US president's 80th birthday, came a few hours after Trump and Iranian officials announced they had reached a preliminary agreement to end the #war.

items.title: Russian strikes kill 11 and set historic cathedral in Kyiv ablaze
items.standfirst: A Ukrainian drone attack in the Russian city of Tula, south of Moscow, killed three people.
items.content: A Ukrainian drone attack in the Russian city of Tula, south of Moscow, killed three people.

clusters.title: Geneva police clash with anti-G7 protesters ahead of summit
clusters.neutral_headline: Protesters clash with police ahead of G7 summit in France
bullet_summary: Reported by Sky News World, BBC World

items.title: Geneva police clash with anti-G7 protesters ahead of summit
items.standfirst: Police in Geneva have fired tear gas and water cannons at protesters who set fire to a Tesla vehicle and smashed windows at a UN agency in protests ahead of Monday's G7 summit in neighbouring France.
items.content: Police in Geneva have fired tear gas and water cannons at protesters who set fire to a Tesla vehicle and smashed windows at a UN agency in protests ahead of Monday's G7 summit in neighbouring France.

items.title: Protesters clash with police ahead of G7 summit in France
items.standfirst: Swiss police deployed a water cannon and fired tear gas to disperse anti-G7 protesters after clashes.
items.content: Swiss police deployed a water cannon and fired tear gas to disperse anti-G7 protesters after clashes.
